text: In this video, we present role of combined laparoscopic and robotic approach in complex reoperative colorectal surgery. This is a sixty-one-year-old male who had a prior right colactomy and endoleostomy with distal colonic mucous fistula for sickle perforation, secondary to severe sigmoid stricture. The patient had known history of chronic diverticulitis and referred to our clinic for definitive management and takedown of the stomas. The preoperative colonoscopy revealed stricture and diverticulosis at the sigmoid column. His ASA score was two, his BMI was twenty seven point seven, he has no comorbidity factors, no other prior operations and no family history for any type of cancer.

In this video, we present role of combined laparoscopic and robotic approach in complex reoperative colorectal surgery. This is a sixty-one-year-old male who had a prior right colactomy and endoleostomy with distal colonic mucous fistula for sickle perforation, secondary to severe sigmoid stricture. The patient had known history of chronic diverticulitis and referred to our clinic for definitive management and takedown of the stomas. The preoperative colonoscopy revealed stricture and diverticulosis at the sigmoid column. His ASA score was two, his BMI was twenty seven point seven, he has no comorbidity factors, no other prior operations and no family history for any type of cancer.

The surgeon inserts the Visiport for initial access into the abdominal cavity, followed by additional trocars under direct vision. Omental adhesions are carefully dissected using endo scissors and a harmonic scalpel, with attention to avoid injury from the vessel sealing device's overheated tip. Scissors are used for precise dissection of interloop adhesions.

The surgeon uses a Visiport for initial access into the abdominal cavity, followed by insertion of additional trocars under direct vision. Omental adhesions are carefully dissected using endo scissors and a harmonic scalpel, with attention to avoid injury from the vessel sealing device's overheated tip. Scissors are used for precise dissection of interloop adhesions.

The surgeon transects the bowel just below the stoma site for the colostomy and ileostomy. Robotic trocars are inserted, and the robot is docked to begin the robotic procedure. The inferior mesenteric vein is clipped and transected. Medial to lateral tissue dissection is performed along embryologic planes for complete mesocolic excision. The lateral dissection is completed, and the splenic flexure is mobilized.

The surgeon transects the bowel just below the stoma site for the colostomy and ileostomy. Robotic trocars are placed, and the robot is docked to begin the robotic procedure. The inferior mesenteric vein is clipped and transected. Medial to lateral tissue dissection is performed along embryologic planes for complete mesocolic excision. The lateral dissection is completed, and the splenic flexure is mobilized.

The surgeon divides the inferior mesenteric artery and checks the transection point between the colon and rectum using ICG fluorescence. The midcolic vessels are transected following the division of the colon and rectum. A heliorectal anastomosis is performed after the resected specimen is removed through the colostomy site.

The surgeon divides the inferior mesenteric artery and checks the transection point between the colon and rectum using ICG fluorescence. The midcolic vessels are transected after separating the colon and rectum. A heliorectal anastomosis is performed, and the resected specimen is removed through the colostomy site.

The operation took three hundred minutes. The blood loss was two hundred fifty milliliters. An acute renal failure developed and managed with medical treatment. The pathology results come up with colonic diverticular disease and the hospital stay after surgery was six days.
